factors.data_availability: very low — PLS is ~1-2% of MND; dedicated PLS biobanks are extremely rare
factors.org_discoverability: low — most PLS specimens are embedded in broader ALS/MND collections, not separately catalogued
factors.biomarker_specificity: low — no PLS-specific biomarker required, but disease confirmation is hard

expected_failure_modes: Pipeline confuses PLS with ALS — PLS is upper motor neuron only; ALS involves both upper and lower motor neurons; most sources catalog both under ALS/MND without separation
Pipeline finds ALS sources and presents them as PLS-eligible without explicit PLS documentation — hard gate violation
Usable N inflated from 529 total C-BIG specimens (all MND diagnoses) to imply 529 PLS donors — actual PLS-confirmed donors are ~20
No second source exists in AminoChain — honest report should state single-source dependency rather than searching for non-existent alternatives
PBMC viability assumed from cryopreserved label without thaw viability confirmation
System may find Project MinE or Answer ALS (genomics studies) and misframe them as biospecimen sources for procurement
